thm:2.4.7.summary: Tilting equivalence at the inverse-limit level (Scholze 7.17 imported): for $X \sim \varprojlim X_i$ with $X$ perfectoid and $X_i$ strongly noetherian adic over $\mathrm{Spa}(K, \mathcal{O}_K)$, the étale topos of $X$ is the inverse limit of those of $X_i$. The technical input from Scholze §7.

thm:4.1.4.summary: Dieudonné module functor: for $R$ f-semiperfect, the Dieudonné functor on $p$-divisible groups up to isogeny is fully faithful. The classification input on the integral side.

thm:4.3.1.summary: Quantitative cokernel control: $\mathrm{coker}(A_{\mathrm{cris}}(S)^* \to \mathbf{M}(G)(A_{\mathrm{cris}}(R)))$ is killed by $p^2$. Sharp integrality of the universal Dieudonné module map.

thm:5.1.4.summary: Quasi-isogeny lift: every $p$-divisible $G/\mathcal{O}_C$ admits a quasi-isogeny $\rho: H \otimes \mathcal{O}_C/p \to G \otimes \mathcal{O}_C/p$ with $H/\bar{\mathbb{F}}_p$. The functor $G_0 \mapsto \mathscr{E}(G_0)$ is fully faithful — Dieudonné classification at the integral $\mathcal{O}_C$ level.

thm:5.2.1.summary: Equivalence between $p$-divisible groups $G/\mathcal{O}_C$ and pairs $(T, W)$ with $T$ free $\mathbb{Z}_p$-module of finite rank and $W \subset T \otimes C(-1)$ a $C$-subspace. The 'Hodge-Tate' classification of $p$-divisible groups via free modules + Hodge filtrations.

thm:6.1.2.summary: Representability of Rapoport–Zink: $\mathrm{Def}_H$ is representable by a formal scheme $\mathcal{M}/W(k)$, with reduced irreducible components proper. Construction of the classical Rapoport–Zink moduli space.

thm:6.2.1.summary: Period map characterization: for fixed $H/k$ ($\dim d, \mathrm{ht} h$), the period map $\pi: \mathcal{M}^{\mathrm{ad}}_\eta \to \mathscr{F}\ell$ is described point-by-point. Connects RZ space to the affine Grassmannian / period domain.

thm:6.3.4.summary: $\mathcal{M}_\infty$ is representable by an adic space over $\mathrm{Spa}(W(k)[1/p], W(k))$, preperfectoid, with $\mathcal{M}_\infty \sim \varprojlim_n \mathcal{M}_n$. The 'infinite-level Rapoport–Zink space' as a perfectoid space — the headline construction.

thm:6.4.1.summary: Cartesian diagram describing $\mathcal{M}_\infty$ via Hodge-Tate periods and universal cover. Geometric description that lifts the algebraic classification of thm:5.2.1 to a geometric isomorphism.

thm:6.5.2.summary: Representability of EL-type RZ: $\mathcal{M}_{\mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{int}}}$ is representable by a formal scheme. EL-type generalization of thm:6.1.2.

thm:6.5.4.summary: $\mathcal{M}_{\mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{int}}, \infty}$ is representable by an adic space over $\mathrm{Spa}(\breve{E}, \mathcal{O}_{\breve{E}})$, preperfectoid, with $\mathcal{M}_{\mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{int}}, \infty} \sim \varprojlim_n \mathcal{M}_{\mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{int}}, n}$. EL-type infinite-level perfectoid.

thm:7.2.3.summary: Duality: a natural $\mathbf{G}(\mathbb{Q}_p) \times \check{\mathbf{G}}(\mathbb{Q}_p)$-equivariant isomorphism $\mathcal{M}_{\mathcal{D}, \infty} \cong \mathcal{M}_{\check{\mathcal{D}}, \infty}$ swapping period maps. Faltings / Fargues / Howe-style duality at infinite level — the Langlands-functoriality outcome.

thm:7.3.1.summary: Drinfeld tower coverings: any finite étale $\mathrm{GL}_n(F)$-equivariant $\tilde{\Omega} \to \Omega$ is dominated by some $\mathcal{M}^{\mathrm{Dr}}_m$. The Drinfeld-side analog of thm:7.2.3, capturing when towers cover all étale extensions.

edges.note: Dieudonné lifting uses fully-faithfulness of the Dieudonné functor on f-semiperfect rings.
Quantitative cokernel control bounds the failure of integrality in the lift.
Hodge-Tate classification refines the integral Dieudonné classification by tracking the Hodge filtration.
Representability of $\mathrm{Def}_H$ uses Dieudonné theory to encode deformation data.
Period map analysis runs on the formal-scheme model produced in 6.1.2.
Infinite-level $\mathcal{M}_\infty$ is built as the inverse limit of finite-level RZ spaces.
Inverse-limit perfectoid criterion (Scholze 7.17) is the technical engine showing the limit is preperfectoid.
Cartesian diagram is the geometric incarnation of the infinite-level RZ space.
Hodge-Tate classification of $p$-divisible groups identifies the diagram's data on points.
EL-type representability extends the basic representability to PEL data.
Infinite-level EL space built on the finite-level formal scheme.
EL version of the basic infinite-level perfectoid statement.
Duality runs at the infinite-level EL space; needs its representability + perfectoid-ness.
Hodge-Tate / period diagram is the symmetric structure that swaps under duality.
Drinfeld-side cofinality is a special case of the duality applied to $\mathrm{GL}_n$ data.

main_results.description: Sections 5.2, 6.3, 7.2. Hodge-Tate classification of $p$-divisible groups; infinite-level RZ space as preperfectoid; duality.

Dieudonne_classification.description: Sections 4–5. Dieudonné theory on f-semiperfect rings, quantitative cokernel control, $\mathcal{O}_C$ classification.

Rapoport_Zink_geometry.description: Section 6. Representability of Rapoport–Zink moduli (basic + EL), period maps, infinite-level limits.

tilting_input.description: Section 2.4. Imported inverse-limit-tilting result from Scholze.

duality_and_drinfeld.description: Section 7. Duality between RZ towers and Drinfeld-side coverings.

Sch.title: Perfectoid spaces
Sch.role: Foundation; thm:2.4.7 imports Scholze's inverse-limit-perfectoid criterion.

Faltings:RZ.title: A relation between two moduli spaces studied by Drinfeld
Faltings:RZ.role: Faltings's original RZ-tower / Drinfeld duality; precursor to thm:7.2.3.

Fargues:RZ.title: L'isomorphisme entre les tours de Lubin-Tate et de Drinfeld
Fargues:RZ.role: Fargues's geometric duality; sharpened version proved here at thm:7.2.3.

RapoportZink.title: Period spaces for p-divisible groups
RapoportZink.role: Original RZ moduli spaces — the formal schemes thm:6.1.2/6.5.2 globalize.
